translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided them, as We had guided Noah before them, and of his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus We reward those who are upright and do good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq (Isaac) and Yaqub (Jacob); We guided all of them; and We guided Nooh before them and of his descendants, Dawud and Sulaiman and Ayyub and Yusuf and Moosa and Haroon; and this is the way We reward the virtuous.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave to him Isaac and Jacob -- each one We guided, And Noah We guided before; and of his seed David and Solomon, Job and Joseph, Moses and Aaron -- even so We recompense the good-doers --

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Is-haq and Ya'qub: each one We guided. And Nuh We had guided afore, and of his progeny Da-ud and Sulaiman and Ayyub and Yusuf and Musa and Harun. And thus We recompense the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh (Noah), and among his progeny Dawud (David), Sulaiman (Solomon), Ayub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses), and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob—each of them We guided. And We guided Noah previously; and from his descendants David, and Solomon, and Job, and Joseph, and Moses, and Aaron. Thus We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon Abraham (offspring) Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob) and each of them did We guide to the right way as We had earlier guided Noah to the right way; and (of his descendants We guided) Da'ud (David) and Sulayman (Solomon), Ayyub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward those who do good.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq and Ya`qub, each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh and among his progeny Dawud, Sulayman, Ayyub, Yusuf, Musa, and Harun. Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob; each of them We guided; and Noah did We guide aforetime; and of his seed (We guided) David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided both; and We guided Noah before them, among his descendants were David and Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron as such, We recompense the gooddoers,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob - all [of them] We guided. And Noah, We guided before; and among his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave (Abraham) Isaac and Jacob. Both had received Our guidance. Noah received Our guidance before Abraham and so did his descendants: David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron. Thus is the reward for the righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave to him Ishaq and Yaqoub; each did We guide, and Nuh did We guide before, and of his descendants, Dawood and Sulaiman and Ayub and Yusuf and Musa and Haroun; and thus do We reward those who do good (to others).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob, each of whom We guided as We had guided Noah before. Among his descendants were David and Solomon, and Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob: all (three) guided: and before him, We guided Noah, and among his progeny, David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron: thus do We reward those who do good:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We have sent revelations to you as We sent revelations to Noah and the prophets (who came) after him; and We sent revelations to Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob, and their offspring, and to Jesus and Job, and to Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and to David We gave the Book of Psalms,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Indeed We sent a divine revelation to you (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessing be upon him) as We did send divine revelations to Nooh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; and We sent divine revelations to Ibrahim (Abraham) and Ismael (Ishmael) and Ishaq (Isaac) and Yaqub (Jacob) and their offspring, and Eisa (Jesus) and Ayyub (Job) and Yunus (Jonah) and Haroon (Aaron) and Sulaiman (Solomon), and We bestowed the Zaboor (the Holy Book) upon Dawud (David).

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: We have revealed to thee as We revealed to Noah, and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, Jesus and Job, Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We have revealed unto thee even as We revealed unto Nuh and the prophets after him, and as We revealed unto Ibrahim and Isma'il and Is-haq and Ya'qub and the tribes, and 'Isa and Ayyub and Yunus and Harun and Sulaiman, and unto Daud We vouchsafed a Scripture.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, We have inspired you (O Muhammad SAW) as We inspired Nuh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; We (also) inspired Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma'il (Ishmael), Ishaque (Isaac), Ya'qub (Jacob), and Al-Asbat [the twelve sons of Ya'qub (Jacob)], 'Iesa (Jesus), Ayub (Job), Yunus (Jonah), Harun (Aaron), and Sulaiman (Solomon), and to Dawud (David) We gave the Zabur (Psalms).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We have inspired you, as We had inspired Noah and the prophets after him. And We inspired Abraham, and Ishmael, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the Patriarchs, and Jesus, and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron, and Solomon. And We gave David the Psalms.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (O Muhammad!) We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the offspring of Jacob, and Jesus and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, We have sent the revelation to you as We sent the revelation to Nuh and the Prophets after him; We (also) sent the revelation to Ibrahim, Isma`il, Ishaq, Ya`qub, and Al-Asbat, (the offspring of the twelve sons of Ya`qub) `Isa, Ayyub, Yunus, Harun, and Sulayman; and to Dawud We gave the Zabur.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! We inspire thee as We inspired Noah and the prophets after him, as We inspired Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and the tribes, and Jesus and Job and Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and as We imparted unto David the Psalms;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and to the Prophets after him, and We revealed to (Prophets) Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the tribes, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and We gave to David the Psalms.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him. And we revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, the Descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and to David We gave the book [of Psalms].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muhammad), We have sent revelations to you just as were sent to Noah and the Prophets who lived after him and to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, his descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon. We gave the Psalms to David.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely We have revealed to you as We revealed to Nuh, and the prophets after him, and We revealed to Ibrahim and Ismail and Ishaq and Yaqoub and the tribes, and Isa and Ayub and Yunus and Haroun and Sulaiman and We gave to Dawood

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We have sent revelation to you [Prophet] as We did to Noah and the prophets who came after him, to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon and David, to whom We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We have sent thee inspiration, as We sent it to Noah and the Messengers after him: we sent inspiration to Abraham, Isma'il, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and solomon, and to David We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Remember Our votary Job because he called to his Lord: "Satan has afflicted me with disease and distress."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Our bondman Ayyub (Job); when he cried out* to his Lord, “The devil has struck me with hardship and pain.” (After seven years of patience.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Remember also Our servant Job; when he called to his Lord, 'Behold, Satan has visited me with weariness and chastisement.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And remember thou Our bondman Ayyub, what time he cried Unto his Lord: verily the Satan hath touched me with affliction and suffering.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And remember Our slave Ayub (Job), when he invoked his Lord (saying): "Verily! Shaitan (Satan) has touched me with distress (by losing my health) and torment (by losing my wealth)!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And mention Our servant Job, when he called out to his Lord, “Satan has afflicted me with hardship and pain.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And remember Our servant Job: when he cried to his Lord: “Behold, Satan has afflicted me with much hardship and suffering.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And remember Our servant Ayyub, when he invoked his Lord (saying): "Verily, Shaytan has afflicted me with distress and torment!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And make mention (O Muhammad) of Our bondman Job, when he cried unto his Lord (saying): Lo! the devil doth afflict me with distress and torment.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Also, remember Our worshiper Job. He called out to his Lord, (saying): 'satan has afflicted me with harm and pain'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And remember Our servant Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, Satan has touched me with hardship and torment."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muhammad), recall Our servant Job. When he prayed to his Lord saying, "Satan has afflicted me with hardship and torment,"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And remember Our servant Ayyub, when he called upon his Lord: The Shaitan has afflicted me with toil and torment.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Bring to mind Our servant Job who cried to his Lord, "Satan has afflicted me with distress and suffering."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Commemorate Our Servant Job. Behold he cried to his Lord: "The Evil One has afflicted me with distress and suffering!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Remember) Job when he called to his Lord: "I am afflicted with distress, and You are the most compassionate of all."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Ayyub (Job), when he called his Lord that, “Hardship has afflicted me, and You are the Most Merciful of all those who have mercy.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Job -- when he called unto his Lord, 'Behold, affliction has visited me, and Thou art the most merciful of the merciful.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Ayyub! recall what time he cried unto his Lord: verily hurt hath touched me, and Thou art the Most Merciful of the mercifuls.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) Ayub (Job), when he cried to his Lord: "Verily, distress has seized me, and You are the Most Merciful of all those who show mercy."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Job, when he cried out to his Lord: “Great harm has afflicted me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We bestowed (the same wisdom, judgement and knowledge) upon Job. Recall, when he cried to his Lord: "Behold, disease has struck me and You are the Most Merciful of those that are merciful."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) Ayyub, when he cried to his Lord: "Verily, distress has seized me, and You are the Most Merciful of all those who show mercy."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Job, when he cried unto his Lord, (saying): Lo! adversity afflicteth me, and Thou art Most Merciful of all who show mercy.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And Job when he called to his Lord: 'Affliction has befallen me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When Job prayed, "Lord, I have been afflicted with hardships. Have mercy on me; You are the Most Merciful of those who have mercy,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Ayub, when he cried to his Lord, (saying): Harm has afflicted me, and Thou art the most Merciful of the merciful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Remember Job when he called on his Lord saying, "I have been afflicted with great distress: but You are the most merciful of the merciful."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And (remember) Job, when He cried to his Lord, "Truly distress has seized me, but Thou art the Most Merciful of those that are merciful."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Remember) Ishmael, Edris and Dhu'l-Kifl. They were men of fortitude,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Ismail, and Idrees, and Zul-Kifl; they were all patiently enduring.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Ishmael, Idris, Dhul Kifl -- each was of the patient,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Isma'il and ldris and Zul-Kifl! each were of the patient.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) Isma'il (Ishmael), and Idris (Enoch) and Dhul-Kifl (Isaiah), all were from among As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Ishmael, and Enoch, and Ezekiel; each was one of the steadfast.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And (We bestowed the same favour) upon Ishmael, Idris and Dhu al-Kifl, for they were all steadfast.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) Isma`il, Idris and Dhul-Kifl: All were from among the patient.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (mention) Ishmael, and Idris, and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were of the steadfast.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And Ishmael, Idris, and ThulKifl (the son of Job) each were patient.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Ismael, Idris and Dhul Kifl all were people of great patience.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Ismail and Idris and Zulkifl; all were of the patient ones;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Remember Ishmael and Idris and Dhul Kifl: they were all patient and steadfast.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And (remember) Isma'il, Idris, and Zul-kifl, all (men) of constancy and patience;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And they were admitted to Our grace. Verily they were among the doers of good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy; indeed they are among those who deserve Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We admitted them into Our mercy; they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We caused them to enter into our mercy, verily they are of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We admitted them to Our Mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. They were among the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy, for they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We admitted them to Our mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We brought them in unto Our mercy. Lo! they are among the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We admitted them to Our Mercy, for they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We encompassed them in Our mercy; they were righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We caused them to enter into Our mercy, surely they were of the good ones.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We admitted them to Our mercy. They were all righteous men.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We admitted them to Our mercy: for they were of the righteous ones.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "Go swiftly to the spring," (We said). "This cold water is for bathing and for drinking."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: We said to him, “Strike the earth with your foot; this cool spring is for bathing and drinking.” (A spring of gushed forth when he struck the earth – this was a miracle.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'Stamp thy foot! This is a laving-place cool, and a drink.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Stamp the ground with thy foot. yonder is water to wash in, cool, and water to drink.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: (Allah said to him): "Strike the ground with your foot: This is a spring of water to wash in, cool and a (refreshing) drink."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Stamp with your foot—here is cool water to wash with, and to drink.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (We commanded him): “Stamp your foot on earth, and here is cool water to wash with and to drink.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: (Allah said to him): "Strike the ground with your foot. This is (a spring of) water to wash in, cool and a drink."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (And it was said unto him): Strike the ground with thy foot. This (spring) is a cool bath and a refreshing drink.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (We said): 'Stamp your foot on the ground, here is cool water with which to wash and a drink'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [So he was told], "Strike [the ground] with your foot; this is a [spring for] a cool bath and drink."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (We answered his prayer, healed his sickness, and told him), "Run on your feet. This is cool water (for you) to wash and drink".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Urge with your foot; here is a cool washing-place and a drink.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We said, "Stamp your foot! Here is cool water for you to wash in and drink,"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (The command was given:) "Strike with thy foot: here is (water) wherein to wash, cool and refreshing, and (water) to drink."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We heard his cry and relieved him of the misery he was in. We restored his family to him, and along with them gave him others similar to them as a grace from Us and reminder for those who are obedient.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: We therefore heard his prayer and removed the adversity that had afflicted him, and We gave him his family and in addition bestowed along with them a similar number, by mercy from Ourselves – and an advice for the people who worship.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So We answered him, and removed the affliction that was upon him, and We gave his people, and the like of them with them, mercy from Us, and a Reminder to those who serve.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So We answered him and We removed that which was with him of the hurt, and We vouchsafed unto him his household and the like thereof along with them, as a mercy from us and a remembrance unto the worshippers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So We answered his call, and We removed the distress that was on him, and We restored his family to him (that he had lost), and the like thereof along with them, as a mercy from Ourselves and a Reminder for all who worship Us.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So We answered him, lifted his suffering, and restored his family to him, and their like with them—a mercy from Us, and a reminder for the worshipers.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We accepted his prayer and removed the affliction from him, and We not only restored to him his family but as many more with them as a mercy from Us and as a lesson to the worshippers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So We answered his call, and We removed the distress that was on him, and We restored his family to him, and the like thereof along with them as a mercy from Ourselves and a Reminder for all those who worship Us.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then We heard his prayer and removed that adversity from which he suffered, and We gave him his household (that he had lost) and the like thereof along with them, a mercy from Our store, and a remembrance for the worshippers;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We answered him and removed his affliction, and We gave his people, and those like them that were with them, mercy from Us, as a reminder to those who worship.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So We responded to him and removed what afflicted him of adversity. And We gave him [back] his family and the like thereof with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers [of Allah].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: "We answered his prayer, relieved him from his hardships, brought his family (back to him) and gave him twice as much property as that (which was destroyed). It was a mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Therefore We responded to him and took off what harm he had, and We gave him his family and the like of them with them: a mercy from Us and a reminder to the worshippers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We heard his prayer and relieved his suffering, We restored to him his family, doubling their number as an act of Our grace, and as a reminder for the worshippers.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So We listened to him: We removed the distress that was on him, and We restored his people to him, and doubled their number,- as a Grace from Ourselves, and a thing for commemoration, for all who serve Us.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We restored his family to him with others similar to them, as a blessing from Us and a reminder for men of wisdom. --

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed his household to him and one more similar to it – as a mercy from Us, and as a remembrance for the people of intellect.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave to him his family, and the like of them with them, as a mercy from us, and a reminder unto men possessed of minds;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed on him his household and along with them the like thereof, out of mercy from us, and a remembrance Unto men of understanding.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We gave him (back) his family, and along with them the like thereof, as a Mercy from Us, and a Reminder for those who understand.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We restored his family for him, and their like with them; as a mercy from Us, and a lesson for those who possess insight.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We granted to him his family and also the like of them, as a mercy from Us, and as a reminder to people of understanding,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We gave him (back) his family, and along with them the like thereof, as a mercy from Us, and a reminder for those who understand.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed on him (again) his household and therewith the like thereof, a mercy from Us, and a memorial for men of understanding.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We restored to him to his family and like those with them, a mercy from Us and a reminder to a nation that understand.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We granted him his family and a like [number] with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for those of understanding.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave him back his family and doubled their number as a blessing from Us and as a reminder to the people of understanding.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave him his family and the like of them with them, as a mercy from Us, and as a reminder to those possessed of understanding.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We restored his family to him, doubling their number as an act of grace from Us, and as a reminder to all who are endowed with insight.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We gave him (back) his people, and doubled their number,- as a Grace from Ourselves, and a thing for commemoration, for all who have Understanding.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "Take a handful of herbs," (We said to him), and apply and rub them, and do not make a mistake." We found him patient in adversity, an excellent devotee, always turning in repentance.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We said, “Take a broom in your hand and strike her with it, and do not break your vow”; We indeed found him patiently enduring; what an excellent bondman! He is indeed most inclined.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and, 'Take in thy hand a bundle of rushes, and strike therewith, and do not fail in thy oath.' Surely We found him a steadfast man. How excellent a servant he was! He was a penitent.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And take in thine hand a handful of twigs, and strike therewith, and break not thine oath. Verily We! We found him patient. How excellent a bondman! Verily he was oft-returning.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And take in your hand a bundle of thin grass and strike therewith (your wife), and break not your oath. Truly! We found him patient. How excellent (a) slave! Verily, he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Take with your hand a bundle, and strike with it, and do not break your oath.” We found him patient. What an excellent servant! He was obedient.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (and We said to him): “Take in your hand a bundle of rushes and strike with it, and do not break your oath.” Indeed We found him steadfast. How excellent a servant (of Ours) he was. Indeed he constantly turned (to his Lord).

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And take in your hand a bundle of thin grass and strike therewith (your wife), and break not your oath." Truly, We found him patient. How excellent a servant! Verily, he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)!

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (it was said unto him): Take in thine hand a branch and smite therewith, and break not thine oath. Lo! We found him steadfast, how excellent a slave! Lo! he was ever turning in repentance (to his Lord).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (We said to him): 'Take a bundle of rushes and strike with it; and do not break your oath' We found him to be patient, a good worshiper and he was penitent.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [We said], "And take in your hand a bunch [of grass] and strike with it and do not break your oath." Indeed, We found him patient, an excellent servant. Indeed, he was one repeatedly turning back [to Allah].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We told him, "Take a handful of straw. Strike your wife with it to fulfill your oath." We found him to be patient. What an excellent servant he was. He was certainly most repenting.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And take in your hand a green branch and beat her with It and do not break your oath; surely We found him patient; most excellent the servant! Surely he was frequent in returning (to Allah).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We said to Him, "Take a handful of twigs in your hand and strike with that but do not break your oath." We found him steadfast. What an excellent servant! He turned constantly to his Lord.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And take in thy hand a little grass, and strike therewith: and break not (thy oath)." Truly We found him full of patience and constancy. How excellent in Our service! ever did he turn (to Us)!

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Abu Huraira: The Prophet said, "Once while Job (Aiyub) was taking a bath in a naked state. Suddenly a great number of gold locusts started falling upon him and he started collecting them in his clothes. His Lord called him, 'O Job! Didn't I make you rich enough to dispense with what you see now?' Job said, 'Yes, O Lord! But I cannot dispense with Your Blessings

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Surely with hardship there is ease.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So indeed with hardship is ease.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So truly with hardship comes ease,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then verily along with every hardship is ease.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So verily, with the hardship, there is relief,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: With hardship comes ease.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Indeed, there is ease with hardship.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, along with every hardship is relief,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But lo! with hardship goeth ease,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Indeed, hardship is followed by ease,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: For indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: After every difficulty there is relief.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely with difficulty is ease.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So, surely with every hardship there is ease;

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So, verily, with every difficulty, there is relief:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: With hardship indeed there is ease.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Indeed with hardship is ease.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: truly with hardship comes ease.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily along with every hardship is ease.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, with the hardship, there is relief (i.e. there is one hardship with two reliefs, so one hardship cannot overcome two reliefs).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: With hardship comes ease.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Most certainly, there is ease with hardship.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, along with every hardship is relief.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! with hardship goeth ease;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: indeed, hardship is followed by ease!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Certainly, after every difficulty there comes relief.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: With difficulty is surely ease.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: surely, with every hardship there is ease.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Verily, with every difficulty there is relief.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O you who believe, seek courage in fortitude and prayer, for God is with those who are patient and persevere.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: O People who Believe! Seek help from patience and prayer; indeed Allah is with those who patiently endure.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: O all you who believe, seek you help in patience and prayer; surely God is with the patient.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: O O Ye who believe! seek help in patience and prayer; verily Allah is with the patient.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: O you who believe! Seek help in patience and As-Salat (the prayer). Truly! Allah is with As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: O you who believe! Seek help through patience and prayers. God is with the steadfast.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: O Believers, seek help with fortitude and Salat, for Allah is with those who show fortitude.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: O you who believe! Seek help in patience and As-Salah (the prayer). Truly, Allah is with As-Sabirin (the patient).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: O ye who believe! Seek help in steadfastness and prayer. Lo! Allah is with the steadfast.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Believers, seek assistance in patience and prayer, Allah is with those who are patient.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: O you who have believed, seek help through patience and prayer. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Believers, help yourselves (in your affairs) through patience and prayer; God is with those who have patience.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O you who believe! seek assistance through patience and prayer; surely Allah is with the patient.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: You who believe, seek help through patience and prayer; surely, God is with the steadfast.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: O ye who believe! seek help with patient perseverance and prayer; for Allah is with those who patiently persevere.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Tell them: "O my creatures who have come to belief, have fear of displeasing your Lord. There is good for those who do good in this world, and productive is God's earth. Only those who persevere will get their reward measureless."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “O my slaves who have accepted faith! Fear your Lord; for those who do good deeds in this world is goodness (in return); and Allah’s earth is spacious; it is the steadfast who will be paid their full reward, without account.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Say: 'My servants who believe, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world good, and God's earth is wide. Surely the patient will be paid their wages in full without reckoning.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Say thou: O Mine bondmen who believe! fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world there is good: and Allah's earth is spacious. Verily the patient shall be paid in full their hire without reckoning.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "O My slaves who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), be afraid of your Lord (Allah) and keep your duty to Him. Good is (the reward) for those who do good in this world, and Allah's earth is spacious (so if you cannot worship Allah at a place, then go to another)! Only those who are patient shall receive their rewards in full, without reckoning."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Say, “O My devotees who have believed, keep your duty to your Lord. For those who do good in this world, is goodness. And God’s earth is vast. The steadfast will be paid their wages in full, without reckoning.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Tell them (O Prophet): “O you servants of Mine who believe, have fear of your Lord. A good end awaits those who did good in this world. Allah's earth is spacious. Verily those who persevere shall be granted their reward beyond all reckoning.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Say: "O My servants who believe, have Taqwa of your Lord. Good is for those who do good in this world, and Allah's earth is spacious! Only those who are patient shall receive their reward in full, without reckoning."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Say: O My bondmen who believe! Observe your duty to your Lord. For those who do good in this world there is good, and Allah's earth is spacious. Verily the steadfast will be paid their wages without stint.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Say: 'My worshipers who believe, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world there is good and the earth of Allah is wide surely, those who are patient will be recompensed in full without count'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Say, "O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good, and the earth of Allah is spacious. Indeed, the patient will be given their reward without account."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Say to My believing servants, "Have fear of your Lord. Those who act righteously in this life will receive good reward. The land of God is vast. God will recompense the deeds of those who have exercised patience, without keeping an account".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Say: O my servants who believe! be careful of (your duty to) your Lord; for those who do good in this world is good, and Allah's earth is spacious; only the patient will be paid back their reward in full without measure.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Say, "[God says] O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world will have a good reward -- and God's earth is spacious. Truly, those who persevere patiently will be requited without measure."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Say: "O ye my servants who believe! Fear your Lord, good is (the reward) for those who do good in this world. Spacious is Allah's earth! those who patiently persevere will truly receive a reward without measure!"
